message:
ci(dist): add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl static artifact for distro-agnostic binary

The current dist matrix produces `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and
`aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu` tarballs. Both link against the host's
glibc, which means the Debian 12 artifact doesn't run on RHEL 9
(different glibc ABI) and vice versa. Users running on Alpine,
Amazon Linux 2023, or any other non-gnu distro can't install a
prebuilt at all — they have to build from source.

Fix: add a statically-linked musl artifact that runs on any
x86_64 Linux regardless of host libc.

.github/workflows/dist.yml:
- Matrix includes both `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` (legacy, kept for
  distros that prefer dynamic linking against glibc) AND the new
  `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` (statically linked, universally
  runnable). Comment block on the matrix entry spells out the
  target distros the static build covers (Debian 12, Ubuntu 22.04,
  RHEL 9, Amazon Linux 2023, Alpine, etc.) so future editors don't
  drop it thinking it's redundant.
- New "Install musl toolchain" step gated on
  `contains(matrix.target, 'musl')` runs `sudo apt-get update &&
  sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools` so `cc` and friends are
  available when cargo calls out to the C linker.
- Thread `RUSTFLAGS: -C target-feature=+crt-static` conditionally
  (`contains(matrix.target, 'musl')`) into the build step so the
  static-crt flag is only applied for musl builds. The ternary
  pattern keeps other targets' `RUSTFLAGS` empty so the gnu /
  arm64 / darwin paths aren't accidentally static-linked.
- aarch64 and all non-linux targets continue to build with their
  prior flags; only the new musl row picks up the new plumbing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -75,6 +75,11 @@ jobs:
         include:
           - os: ubuntu-latest
             target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
+          # Statically-linked musl build — runs on any x86_64 Linux regardless
+          # of host glibc (Debian 12, Ubuntu 22.04, RHEL 9, Amazon Linux 2023,
+          # Alpine, etc.). Keeps the gnu artifact for distros that prefer it.
+          - os: ubuntu-latest
+            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
           - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
             target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
           # x86_64-apple-darwin removed: ort-sys lacks prebuilt ONNX
@@ -108,11 +113,19 @@ jobs:
         with:
           targets: ${{ matrix.target }}
 
+      - name: Install musl toolchain
+        if: contains(matrix.target, 'musl')
+        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools
+
       - name: Build release binaries
         # --no-default-features --features portable: exclude hybrid/fastembed
         # (ONNX Runtime ships AVX2-only static libs → SIGILL on older x86 CPUs).
         # Portable keeps s3fifo + tantivy-engine; hybrid can still be enabled
         # by users who build from source with default features.
+        env:
+          # Statically link glibc-free musl builds so the binary runs on any
+          # x86_64 Linux regardless of host libc. Leaves other targets alone.
+          RUSTFLAGS: ${{ contains(matrix.target, 'musl') && '-C target-feature=+crt-static' || '' }}
         run: |
           cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }} -p mcp-agent-mail -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --no-default-features --features portable
